document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical evaluation report for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors under EU MDR clinical evaluation requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports conformity assessment for safety, performance, benefit-risk ratio, and acceptability of undesirable side-effects under Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
keyPoints: The report was made in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Annex XIV.
The devices are undergoing initial CE-marking and are not placed on the market yet.
The device is classified as Class IIa under Rule 17 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
The report concludes compliance with MDR GSPR 1, GSPR 2, and GSPR 8.
The clinical evaluation update frequency is every two years.

concepts.name: Clinical Evaluation
concepts.evidence: This report is made in accordance with the MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Annex XIV.

concepts.name: General Safety and Performance Requirements
concepts.pageTarget: MDR General Safety and Performance Requirements
concepts.evidence: we conclude that the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, is compliance to General Safety and Performance Requirements 1... General Safety and Performance Requirement 2... and General Safety and Performance Requirement 8

concepts.name: Benefit-risk ratio
concepts.evidence: Benefit-risk of the product was analysed and we have demonstrated such profile of this product is acceptable based on the state of art in the medical fields concerned.

concepts.name: Equivalence
concepts.evidence: We have demonstrated equivalence according to the Demonstration of Equivalence requirements of MEDDEV 2.7 / 1 Rev.4

concepts.name: Post Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: We researched and summarized the total experience with the device and PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow-up) of the equivalent device in the market.

concepts.name: Post-market Surveillance
concepts.evidence: To evaluate residual risk on our device, long term study through Post-market Surveillance is necessary.

concepts.name: Class IIa
concepts.evidence: Therefore, this device is classified as Class IIa in accordance with the rule 17 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The report evaluates the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector for safety, performance, benefit-risk ratio, and undesirable side-effects.
evidence.evidenceText: The primary purpose of this report is to evaluate conformity assessment with requirement on safety and performance and acceptable benefit-risk ratio (Regulation (EU) 2017/745 GSPR 1), and acceptability of undesirable side-effects (Regulation (EU) 2017/745 GSPR 8).

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report was prepared under MEDDEV 2.7/1 and EU MDR Annex XIV.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is made in accordance with the MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Annex XIV.

evidence.claim: The device models under evaluation are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd. in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer Name: H&abyz Co., Ltd. Address: 2-Dong, 41-16, Cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-eup, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, 17118, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: The device is undergoing initial CE-marking and is not placed on the market yet.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is undergoing initial CE-marking. This product is not placed on the market yet.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The device is not intended for mammography, infants, or pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: The device is a reusable skin-contacting active diagnostic and monitoring device.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is the device contacting skin. Single use / reusable Reusable. It is an active device intended for diagnosis and monitoring.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Classification

evidence.claim: The device is classified as Class IIa under Rule 17 of Annex VIII to Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: Devices specifically intended for recording of diagnostic images generated by X-ray radiation are classified as class IIa. Therefore, this device is classified as Class IIa in accordance with the rule 17 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The report identifies DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 as equivalent CE-marked devices.
evidence.evidenceText: We indicated DRX-1 and CXDI-70C and CXDI-31 as equivalence products. DRX-1 of Carestream Health (CE marked by NB 0086); CXDI-70C of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197); CXDI-31 of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197).

evidence.claim: The report concludes that the equivalent devices are equivalent to the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector despite non-significant differences.
evidence.evidenceText: we concluded that the CE indicators ‘DRX-1’ and ‘CXDI-70C’ and ‘CXDI-31’ are equivalent to ‘Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector’, although our products are not exactly the same in appearance (see 1.1), Scintillator (1.6), etc.

evidence.claim: The report concludes conformity with MDR GSPR 1, GSPR 2, and GSPR 8.
evidence.evidenceText: we conclude that the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, is compliance to General Safety and Performance Requirements 1... General Safety and Performance Requirement 2... and General Safety and Performance Requirement 8
evidence.pageTarget: MDR General Safety and Performance Requirements

evidence.claim: No known side effects are stated for the A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW have no known side effects.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Safety

evidence.claim: The device has an intended service life of approximately five years.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Specifications

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report update frequency is every two years.
evidence.evidenceText: The frequency of updates for the clinical evaluation report is every two years.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text is primarily EU MDR and CE-marking focused.
Authority is not fully explicit as a single regulator; text references Regulation (EU) 2017/745, CE MDR, and 3EC (NB).
Information materials table contains placeholder Korean date text '날짜' for label and user manual revision dates.

Potential internal conflict: classification checklist marks 'Device intended for recording of X-ray diagnostic images?' as 'N', while the classification rationale applies MDR Rule 17 for devices intended for recording diagnostic X-ray images.
Preprocessed text includes OCR or extraction artifacts such as repeated phrases, misspellings, and possible standard typo 'EN 62222-1-1:2015' where the complied standards list uses EN 62220-1-1:2015.
